steps.title: Write the factors
steps.content:
Zeros of $-4$, $5$, and $9$ mean the polynomial has factors $x+4$, $x-5$, and $x-9$.
$$h(x)=(x+4)(x-5)(x-9)$$

steps.title: Use the constant term
steps.content:
In $h(x)=x^3+ax^2+bx+c$, the constant term is $h(0)$. Substitute $x=0$ into the factored form.
$$c=(0+4)(0-5)(0-9)$$

steps.title: Multiply the constants
steps.content: Evaluate the product. $$4\cdot(-5)\cdot(-9)=(-20)(-9)=180$$ The answer is 180.
